name: Axial Mode Helical (wire)
description: A helical antenna operating in axial mode where the circumference is approximately one wavelength, producing circularly polarized radiation along the helix axis. The antenna consists of a wire wound in a helical coil above a ground plane, providing excellent circular polarization and moderate gain.

circumference.name: Circumference
circumference.description: Circumference of the helical coil, typically one wavelength for axial mode

pitch_angle.name: Pitch Angle
pitch_angle.description: Angle between the helix wire and a plane perpendicular to the helix axis

number_turns.name: Number of Turns
number_turns.description: Total number of helical turns, affects gain and bandwidth

wire_diameter.name: Wire Diameter
wire_diameter.description: Diameter of the helical wire conductor

ground_plane_diameter.name: Ground Plane Diameter
ground_plane_diameter.description: Diameter of the ground plane reflector

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves determining the helical dimensions for axial mode operation, ensuring proper impedance matching and circular polarization characteristics.

steps.title: Determine Operating Frequency
steps.description: Select center frequency and bandwidth requirements

steps.title: Calculate Circumference
steps.description: Set circumference to approximately one wavelength at center frequency

steps.title: Determine Number of Turns
steps.description: Calculate turns based on desired gain and bandwidth

steps.title: Calculate Pitch Angle
steps.description: Determine optimal pitch angle for axial mode operation

steps.title: Size Ground Plane
steps.description: Design ground plane for proper radiation characteristics

steps.title: Select Wire Diameter
steps.description: Choose wire diameter for mechanical stability and bandwidth

steps.title: Calculate Input Impedance
steps.description: Determine feed point impedance for matching network design

equations.resonantFrequency: f0 = c/C where C is circumference

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Use transmission line model with helical geometry corrections and ground plane effects
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Apply Hansen-Woodyard condition for endfire array with helical phase progression

references: Balanis, C.A. 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', Chapter 9
Stutzman, W.L. & Thiele, G.A. 'Antenna Theory and Design', Chapter 8
Kraus, J.D. 'Antennas for All Applications', Chapter 7
